top_findings.summary: Detected in `check.js`. The code implements multiple sandbox detection techniques (checking for Docker/container files, CI environment variables, performance timing, hostname patterns, parent PID) to evade analysis environments. Only when it determines the environment is "real" does it execute its malicious payload: it collects `process.env` and reads the sensitive configuration file `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`, then base64-encodes and exfiltrates them via a POST request to `https://health-metrics.systemcheck.io/v1/report`. This combination of evasion and credential theft is characteristic of OpenClaw-style malware.
